name: Beer
description: Brews, breweries, ingredients, and beer culture

questions.q: Which beer style was first brewed in the Bohemian city of Plzeň in 1842?

source.original_q: Which legendary beer style was born in the Czech Republic in 1842 and still slays?
source.notes: Josef Groll brewed the first batch October 5, 1842 at what is now Pilsner Urquell Brewery.

questions.q: Founded in 1040, which Bavarian brewery is widely recognized as the world's oldest continuously operating brewery?

source.original_q: Which brewery has been crafting beer since before Shakespeare could spell "ale"?
source.notes: Licensed by the City of Freising in 1040. Weltenburg Abbey (1050) also claims the title.

questions.q: First held in 1982 and now based in Denver, what is the largest ticketed beer festival in the United States?

source.original_q: What beer-lover's dream event turns Denver into hop heaven once a year?
source.notes: Founded by Charlie Papazian; started in Boulder 1982, moved to Denver 1984. Hosted by Brewers Association.

questions.q: By global consumption volume, what is the most widely consumed alcoholic beverage in the world?

source.original_q: What fizzy drink has claimed the global throne as the world's most sipped alcohol?
source.notes: Beer accounts for roughly 75% of global alcohol consumption by volume.

questions.q: By volume, what is the main ingredient in beer?

source.original_q: What ingredient makes up most of your beer-besides your weekend plans?
source.notes: Typically 90-95% of beer is water.

questions.q: Which ingredient provides most of beer's bitterness and aromatic character?

source.original_q: What tiny green cone gives beer its signature bite and makes IPA fans feel superior?
source.notes: Humulus lupulus. Alpha acids provide bitterness; essential oils provide aroma.

questions.q: Which beer ingredient is primarily responsible for the beverage's color, body, and fermentable sugars?

source.original_q: What ingredient is beer's main flavor artist-handling color, taste, and good vibes?
source.notes: Kilning/roasting level of malt determines color from pale gold to jet black.

questions.q: In brewing, which step converts the starches in malted grain into fermentable sugars?

source.original_q: What magical step in brewing turns starch into sugar-basically beer's fairy godmother moment?
source.notes: Enzymes (amylase) break down starch at 148-158°F during the mash.

questions.q: The word 'lager' comes from the German verb 'lagern.' What does it mean?

source.original_q: What German word means "to store," like what you tell yourself every time you buy a 6-pack for "later"?
source.notes: Lagers are cold-stored (lagered) for weeks to months during conditioning.

questions.q: Which yeast species is used for top-fermentation in ales?

source.original_q: What yeast floats to the top like a drama queen during fermentation?
source.notes: S. pastorianus is the lager yeast. Brettanomyces is used in wild/sour ales.

questions.q: What does the beer-style abbreviation 'IPA' stand for?

source.original_q: Which beer style is like a hop explosion in your mouth, in the best way possible?
source.notes: Originally brewed in England for export to British India in the 18th-19th centuries.

questions.q: On a beer label, what does 'IBU' stand for?

source.original_q: What's the scale that tells you how bitter your beer-and maybe your ex-is?
source.notes: Measures iso-alpha acids from hops. Typical range: 5 (light lager) to 100+ (double IPA).

questions.q: Which Belgian beer style undergoes spontaneous fermentation using wild, airborne yeast?

source.original_q: What beer is brewed with wild yeast and a wild heart in Belgium?
source.notes: Brewed in the Pajottenland region near Brussels. Coolship exposed to open air overnight.

questions.q: Which German beer style gets its smoky flavor from malt dried over an open flame?

source.original_q: Which beer tastes like it just walked out of a barbecue?
source.notes: From Bamberg, Bavaria. 'Rauch' = smoke in German. Schlenkerla is the famous example.

questions.q: Which Belgian wheat beer is traditionally brewed with coriander and orange peel?

source.original_q: Which beer pops with bubbles, citrus, and spice-and could double as brunch's best friend?
source.notes: Hoegaarden is the classic example. 'Wit' = white in Dutch/Flemish.

questions.q: Which variety of stout gets its characteristic sweetness from the addition of lactose?

source.original_q: Which dessert-like beer is brewed with lactose and tastes like it's giving you a hug?
source.notes: Also called sweet stout or cream stout. Lactose is unfermentable by brewer's yeast.

questions.q: Which beer style's name combines a grain and a wine, reflecting its unusually high alcohol content?

source.original_q: Which bold beer has a high ABV and is often aged like fine whiskey?
source.notes: Typically 8-12% ABV. English and American substyles. Often cellar-aged.

questions.q: What term describes beers intentionally brewed at lower alcohol levels for extended, comfortable drinking?

source.original_q: Which beer is like your chill buddy-low alcohol, easy to hang with all night?
source.notes: Generally under 5% ABV. Term traces to British 'drinking sessions' during WWI work breaks.

questions.q: Which dark English beer style, popular since the 1700s, features roasted malt flavors and was a favorite of London's river and street porters?

source.original_q: Which brew has deep, roasted vibes and shows up at trivia night like it owns the place?
source.notes: Originated in London early 1700s. Named for street and river porters who drank it.

questions.q: Which beer style uses rye grain to add a distinctive spicy, peppery character?

source.original_q: Which beer brings the spice with rye and a little bite in every sip?
source.notes: German Roggenbier is the traditional example. American rye IPAs are a modern variant.
